title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
summary: A tutorial on building a self-improving knowledge base for the Hermes AI agent using Obsidian wiki, Claude, and NotebookLM. The workflow ingests meeting notes, emails, and research via automated cron jobs with Granola, implementing retrieval-augmented generation principles inspired by Andrej Karpathy's LLM wiki methodology to overcome LLM context limitations.
writeup: Jack Roberts walks through building a self-improving knowledge base for a Hermes AI agent, using an Obsidian wiki connected to Claude and NotebookLM. The setup automatically ingests meetings, emails, and research through Granola and cron jobs, so the wiki grows without manual upkeep. It applies Andrej Karpathy's LLM wiki principles to give agents persistent memory that spans documents. The takeaway: a structured, continuously updated wiki can serve as durable, growing context for an AI agent.
